contextual_info: Height of base is 2.5 parts of total temple height in Subhadraka temple.
Height of base is 3/24 of total temple height in Svastika temple.
Height of base is 3/28 of total temple height.
Socle height can equal base height.
Attic height is twice base height.
Ground floor height equals base height.
Linga height derived from base dimensions.
Base height is half pillar height minus one-sixth pillar height.
Base height is 1/7 of upper storey pillar height.
Entablature height is 3/4 of base height in a malika.
Provisional pedestal dimensions equal original base dimensions.
Added storey body height is 7.5 parts, base is 2.5 parts.
Uragabandha upana height is 5/18 of total base height.
Uragabandha kumuda height is 6/18 of total base height.
Padabandha upana height is 8/24 of total base height.
Pratikrama upana height is 7/21 of total base height.
Altar first plinth height is 1/16 of total height.
Padabandha kumuda height is 7/24 of total base height.
Pratikrama kumuda height is 6/21 of total base height.
Altar torus height is 3/16 of total height.
Pattika height is 1/3 of frieze height.
Altar listel height is 1/16 of total height.
Stereobate height is 1/3 of attic height.
Padmakesara ksepana height is 6/26 of total base height.
Ksepana height is 1/9 of bell capital height.
Padabandha kantha height is 3/24 of total base height.
Padabandha kampa height is 1/24 of total base height.
Bell capital kantha height is 1/9 of total height.
Urethra height is 10/16 of Siva element height.
Uragabandha pattika height is 2/18 of total base height.
Padabandha pattika height is 3/24 of total base height.
Stereobate height is 1/10 of aedicula pillar height.
Pillar height is greater than double base height.
Base is a foundational architectural element.
Specific base types are designated for gods, kings, or celestial beings.
Base types have prescribed height divisions (e.g., 22, 24, 18 parts).
Base can be decorated with motifs like lotus.
Foundation deposits include items like golden vases and lotuses.
Base dimensions are often modular, related to pillar width.
Base shape can be square, rectangular, or apsidal.
Base components have specific proportional relationships.
